items.q: What does a 'large scale' map mean?
items.correct: A large-scale map covers a small area in high detail (e.g. 1:25,000 OS map of one town); a small-scale map covers a large area in less detail (e.g. 1:1,000,000 world atlas).
items.misconception: A large-scale map shows a large area.
items.random_wrong: Large scale means the map is physically big.
items.why: This is counter-intuitive but mathematically logical: 1/25,000 is a larger fraction than 1/1,000,000. Students reverse the terminology and consistently mark themselves down on map skill questions.

items.q: What is the enhanced greenhouse effect?
items.correct: The strengthening of the natural greenhouse effect by human activities (mainly burning fossil fuels) which add CO2 and other gases to the atmosphere.
items.misconception: The greenhouse effect is entirely man-made and harmful.
items.random_wrong: It is the hole in the ozone layer letting in more sun.
items.why: The natural greenhouse effect is essential for life — without it Earth would be -18 degrees C. The problem is enhancement. Students also confuse it with the (separate) ozone hole.

items.q: What is the difference between biotic and abiotic components of an ecosystem?
items.correct: Biotic components are all living organisms (plants, animals, microbes); abiotic components are non-living factors (climate, soil, water, light, rock).
items.misconception: Biotic means animals and abiotic means plants.
items.random_wrong: Biotic and abiotic both refer to types of soil.
items.why: Plants are biotic, not abiotic. Students sort by familiarity rather than living vs non-living. The distinction matters because abiotic factors set the conditions for which biotic communities develop.

items.q: Approximately what is current atmospheric CO₂ concentration (ppm)?
items.why: Correct answer: 420. “280” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: What does the abbreviation IPCC stand for?
items.why: It's Intergovernmental Panel on Climate Change. “International Pollution Control Committee” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.

items.q: What was the approximate pre-industrial atmospheric CO₂ concentration (ppm)?
items.why: Exact answer: 280. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
